rationale: The citing paper uses Dinner et al. (2014) for a core conceptual link in its framework: offline/TV advertising can raise online search, and advertising effects can propagate to sales through search. It also cites Dinner et al. in the model setup as precedent for AdStock-based dynamic specification of advertising effects. This goes beyond a passing background mention because the cited paper helps justify both hypothesis development and key modeling choices in the empirical design.
relation_description: The citing study builds on Dinner et al. by adopting the advertising-to-search-to-sales logic and related dynamic modeling conventions, then adding ad-content and product-positioning moderators. In this sense, Dinner et al. provides an empirical and methodological base that the new paper develops further.
